name: Nx
what_it_does: Smart build system and monorepo tool with deep task graph analysis, incremental computation caching, and a rich plugin ecosystem supporting React, Angular, Node.js, Go, Rust, and more. Nx Cloud provides remote caching and distributed task execution with a REST API. Includes powerful code generators (nx generate) for scaffolding.
use_cases: Remote cache hit/miss analytics and configuration via Nx Cloud REST API
Triggering distributed task execution (DTE) across multiple CI agents via Nx Cloud
Code generation for new libraries, applications, and components via nx generate
Dependency graph analysis to identify affected projects in a monorepo
Managing a large polyglot monorepo with mixed tech stacks
not_for: Single-package projects (significant overhead with minimal benefit)
Teams that need a lightweight task runner without code generation opinions
Projects that want zero configuration overhead (Turborepo is simpler)
Non-technical teams - Nx has a steeper learning curve than Turborepo
best_when: An agent is managing a large polyglot monorepo and needs distributed task execution, remote caching with analytics, and code generation capabilities via Nx Cloud's API.
avoid_when: You have a simple JavaScript-only monorepo where Turborepo's lighter approach would suffice, or the configuration overhead is prohibitive.

auth.notes: Nx Cloud uses access tokens set via NX_CLOUD_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable or nx.json configuration. Tokens are workspace-scoped. Personal access tokens available for individual developer machines.

pricing.free_tier_limits: OSS Nx CLI is fully free. Nx Cloud free tier: up to $0 for individual workspaces with limited saved time.
pricing.paid_tiers: Nx Cloud Pro from $35/mo per workspace, Business and Enterprise with custom pricing for DTE and advanced features
pricing.notes: OSS CLI has no cost. Nx Cloud free tier is limited but usable for small teams. Distributed task execution (DTE) - the most powerful feature - requires a paid plan. Pricing is per-workspace not per-user.

performance.rate_limits: Nx Cloud API: not publicly documented; designed for CI orchestration use cases

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Nx CLI output is well-formatted with task graphs and cache indicators. Errors from failed tasks show output inline. Generator errors can be cryptic when schema validation fails. Nx Cloud dashboard provides richer error context than CLI output alone.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Task outputs are content-hashed and cached. Same inputs always produce the same cache hit/miss behavior. Nx Cloud run IDs are unique per execution. Code generators are not idempotent - running the same generator twice creates conflicts.
known_agent_gotchas: NX_CLOUD_ACCESS_TOKEN must be set; without it Nx silently falls back to local cache with no warning
nx.json configuration schema has changed between major versions; agents must target the correct schema version
The affected detection algorithm requires a complete git history; shallow clones break --affected flags
Nx generators are opinionated about directory structure; agent-generated paths may conflict with conventions
Plugin versions must be kept in sync with the core nx version to avoid compatibility errors
Distributed task execution requires multiple CI agents to be configured in the orchestration layer - not just an API call

score_rationale: Nx is the most feature-complete monorepo tool with genuine cloud API integration via Nx Cloud, distributed task execution, and a rich plugin ecosystem. Agent-readiness is moderate: the Nx Cloud REST API enables build orchestration and cache analytics, code generation is powerful for scaffolding, and documentation is generally accurate. Scores are limited by the lack of a well-documented public API spec, complex configuration that varies by plugin, strict version synchronization requirements between nx and plugins, and DTE requiring paid plans. Nx is more capable than Turborepo but also more complex for agents to configure correctly.
